findings.title: Multiple individual type assertions in search result mapping
findings.code_snippet:
return results.map(row => ({
  id: row.id as string,
  source: row.source as string,
  content: row.content as string,
  createdAt: row.createdAt as number,
  _distance: row._distance as number,
}))
findings.description: Five individual type assertions on each field of the search result. This is verbose and each assertion silently overrides the type checker. A single typed cast on the row or a small validation/transform function would be clearer and consolidate the type boundary to one location.
findings.suggestion:
BEFORE:
return results.map(row => ({
  id: row.id as string,
  source: row.source as string,
  content: row.content as string,
  createdAt: row.createdAt as number,
  _distance: row._distance as number,
}))

AFTER:
return results.map(row => row as unknown as VectorSearchResult)

WHY: Consolidates 5 type assertions to 1 cast at the boundary. If the row shape changes, you update one place instead of five.

findings.title: Double cast through unknown to null the db reference
findings.code_snippet: this.db = null as unknown as lancedb.Connection
findings.description: The close() method nulls the db reference using a double cast (null -> unknown -> Connection). This is a type system escape hatch that hides the fact that the field could be null after close(). Any method called after close() will throw a confusing runtime error on null rather than a clear 'store is closed' message.

findings.title: Inconsistent result type handling between stores
findings.description: getRecentConversations directly casts the result to Conversation[] (line 64), while getTodos (lines 85-95) uses an intermediate raw type with manual mapping for the boolean conversion. The approaches are inconsistent -- one trusts the DB shape entirely, the other transforms it. The Conversation[] cast is correct because all fields map directly, but the pattern difference may confuse future readers about when transformation is needed.
findings.suggestion:
Add a brief inline comment on getRecentConversations explaining why no mapping is needed (all columns alias to match the interface), so the contrast with getTodos is intentional and documented.

BEFORE:
return stmt.all(limit) as Conversation[]

AFTER:
// All columns alias directly to Conversation fields; no transform needed
return stmt.all(limit) as Conversation[]

WHY: Makes the intentional difference between this method and getTodos (which requires boolean conversion) explicit for future readers.

positive_observations: All functions are short (under 15 lines) with low cyclomatic complexity (1-2), well under thresholds
Consistent use of guard clauses and early returns (createTable existing check, search empty table check)
Clear separation between VectorStore (LanceDB) and DocumentStore (SQLite) with distinct responsibilities
initTables uses a single db.exec() call for all schema setup, clean and atomic
Parameterized queries used throughout DocumentStore -- no SQL string concatenation
